title: LLM: Manual QA (T-13)
description: Boot example app, exercise all LLM features end-to-end
close_reason: Manual QA complete. All LLM features verified via MCP WebSocket: availability, model info, generate, session create/send/context/destroy, summarize, rewrite. Error handling works correctly (LLM_SESSION_NOT_FOUND for destroyed/nonexistent sessions). Fixed 4 bugs during QA: (1) cargo:rustc-link-arg doesn't propagate from rlib - fixed with static archive, (2) Swift runtime rpath missing - fixed with rustc-link-arg for tests and example build.rs, (3) Swift JSONEncoder camelCase mismatch - removed convertToSnakeCase, (4) parse_swift_json didn't detect Swift error envelope - added error field check. All 5 existing e2e tests still pass. 67 unit tests pass. Standards clean.

title: LLM: Build system Swift bridge (T-03)
description: Update build.rs for Swift compilation on macOS 26

title: LLM: Tauri commands & dispatch (T-02)

title: LLM: Rust types & error variants (T-01)
description: Add all LLM types to models/mod.rs: LlmAvailability, LlmModelInfo, LlmModelCapabilities, LlmGenerateOptions, LlmFinishReason, LlmUsage, LlmGenerateResult, LlmStreamEvent, LlmSessionOptions, LlmSummarizeOptions/Result, LlmRewriteTone/Options/Result. Add language_model to Capabilities. Add 4 error variants to error.rs.
design: Follow existing derive patterns. Tagged enum for LlmStreamEvent.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test passes, npm run standards passes

title: Rewire plugin to consume device-ai
description: Reduce the root crate to Tauri command registration, state, channel bridging, and mobile host registration glue. Route desktop and shared functionality through the new library.
acceptance_criteria: The plugin consumes device-ai instead of owning backend logic, and the JS-facing plugin API remains stable.

title: Extract Windows backend into device-ai
description: Move the Windows speech, OCR, capability, and related helper logic into crates/device-ai with target-specific dependencies and clear unsupported-feature behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Windows functionality is owned by device-ai and the root plugin no longer contains the Windows backend implementation.

title: Extract macOS backend into device-ai
description: Move the macOS speech, vision, language ID, and FoundationModels implementation plus related helpers into crates/device-ai and keep backend build responsibilities with the library.
acceptance_criteria: macOS functionality is owned by device-ai and the root plugin no longer contains the macOS backend implementation.

title: Design device-ai public API
description: Extract shared types, errors, capabilities, and request/response shapes into an ergonomic Rust-first API. The plugin should adapt to the library surface instead of the library mirroring IPC-oriented shapes.
acceptance_criteria: The library exposes a coherent Rust API with strong types, builders/helpers, and an explicit capability-oriented surface.

title: Bootstrap workspace and device-ai crate
description: Convert the repository into a Cargo workspace, add crates/device-ai, define the initial module layout, and establish the public crate manifest, feature layout, and docs scaffolding.
acceptance_criteria: The repo builds as a workspace and the new device-ai crate exists as the public Rust library entry point.

title: Extract Tauri-agnostic device-ai crate
description: Create a crates/device-ai library that owns the native on-device AI implementation and make the root crate a thin Tauri plugin adapter. Exclude browser/Web APIs and preserve plugin parity while improving the direct Rust API.
acceptance_criteria: A tracked migration plan exists for workspace bootstrap, API design, backend extraction, plugin rewiring, mobile seam work, and verification.

title: LLM: Documentation (T-12)
description: Update README.md, architecture.md, lib.rs

title: LLM: Example app tab (T-10)
description: Add LLM tab to example App.svelte

description: Add LLM handlers to DeviceAiPlugin.swift

title: LLM: Windows WinRT integration (T-06)
description: Research/implement Windows LLM via windows crate or stubs

title: macOS face landmarks detection returns None (stub)
description: In src/macos.rs detect_faces_vision(), when detect_landmarks is true, a VNDetectFaceLandmarksRequest is created and executed, but the code at lines 1160-1170 always returns None for landmarks with a comment 'Placeholder as mapping all points is verbose'. The README documents face landmarks as a working feature, and the example app UI has a detectLandmarks toggle.
design: Map the VNFaceLandmarks2D points from observation.landmarks() into the FaceLandmarks struct. The VNFaceLandmarks2D object has properties like leftEye, rightEye, nose, outerLips, etc. that return VNFaceLandmarkRegion2D objects. Each region has normalizedPoints (array of CGPoint). Map these into the existing FaceLandmarks type fields (leftEye, rightEye, nose, mouth, leftEar, rightEar as Vec of Point).
acceptance_criteria: 1. When detectLandmarks=true, actual landmark points are returned. 2. cargo test passes. 3. npm run standards passes. 4. Manual QA: test face detection with landmarks enabled in example app, verify non-null landmark data returned.

title: vision.ts missing isTauri() guards for non-web-fallback functions
description: In guest-js/vision.ts, recognizeText(), detectFaces(), and classifyImage() always call invoke() without first checking isTauri(). detectBarcodes() correctly checks isTauri() and falls back to web APIs. The other three functions have no web fallback but will crash with an unclear error when invoked outside a Tauri context (e.g. in a browser during development).
design: Add isTauri() checks to recognizeText(), detectFaces(), and classifyImage(). When not in Tauri, throw a clear DeviceAiError with code NOT_IN_TAURI_CONTEXT explaining that these features require native platform APIs and are not available in the browser. Import the error helper from errors.ts.
acceptance_criteria: 1. All three functions throw a clear error when not in Tauri context. 2. Functions work normally when in Tauri context. 3. npm run standards passes.

title: get_capabilities command bypasses DeviceAiApis struct
description: The get_capabilities IPC command in commands/mod.rs calls get_platform_capabilities() directly instead of going through app.device_ai_apis().get_capabilities() like all other commands. On Windows, this means the static capabilities from capabilities.rs are returned (always reports everything as available) instead of windows::get_capabilities() which does runtime detection via SpeechRecognizer::new(), SpeechSynthesizer::new(), and OcrEngine::TryCreateFromUserProfileLanguages(). This can report features as available when they are not actually present on the system.
design: Change commands/mod.rs get_capabilities() to use app.device_ai_apis().get_capabilities() instead of get_platform_capabilities(), matching the pattern of all other commands (speech.rs, vision.rs, text.rs all use DeviceAiApisExt). Remove the unused get_platform_capabilities import.
acceptance_criteria: 1. get_capabilities command uses app.device_ai_apis().get_capabilities(). 2. cargo test passes. 3. npm run standards passes. 4. Manual QA: boot example app, verify capabilities are reported correctly.

title: Remove Rust duplicate code
description: Refactor duplicate Rust clone groups reported by the duplicate scanner in capabilities, Windows LLM stubs, the device-ai example, and Swift runtime build helper code.
close_reason: Resolved duplicate Rust clone groups and verified with duplicate scan plus standards.

title: Add docs, examples, and verification coverage
description: Add direct Rust examples, update README and sample-app guidance, add sample assets as needed, and define manual verification steps that cover both the library and plugin surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Both the direct Rust crate and the sample app have documented verification paths for the supported native AI capabilities.

title: Establish Android and iOS backend seam
description: Define the backend seam that lets the new library own the shared contract while the plugin continues to delegate to the existing Kotlin and Swift implementations until full mobile extraction is decided.
acceptance_criteria: The migration has an explicit mobile integration path that preserves current plugin behavior without leaving desktop core logic in the plugin crate.

title: Remove dead code: unused provider traits, request types, and Cargo.toml cleanup
description: Multiple dead code items: 1) src/providers/ directory contains trait definitions (SpeechRecognitionProvider, SpeechSynthesisProvider, VisionProvider, TextProvider) that are never implemented — all platform code uses direct functions. They have #[allow(dead_code)] and #[allow(unused_imports)] annotations. 2) models/mod.rs has unused request types (SpeechRecognizeRequest, SpeechSynthesizeRequest, VisionRecognizeTextRequest, TextIdentifyLanguageRequest, TextTranslateRequest) only used in their own tests. 3) Cargo.toml has base64 listed twice (line 21 top-level and line 38 macOS-specific). 4) Cargo.toml authors = ["You"]. 5) src/test_ci.rs imports CIDetector and calls .class() — dead code build check.
design: 1) Delete entire src/providers/ directory and remove mod providers from lib.rs. 2) Remove unused request types from models/mod.rs and their associated tests. 3) Remove top-level base64 dep from Cargo.toml (keep macOS-specific one). 4) Update Cargo.toml authors. 5) Remove test_ci.rs and its mod declaration. 6) Ensure no code references removed items.
acceptance_criteria: 1. No dead code warnings for removed items. 2. cargo test passes. 3. npm run standards passes (including clippy). 4. No #[allow(dead_code)] or #[allow(unused_imports)] needed for removed code.

title: Fix README permissions section to use Tauri v2 format
description: README.md Permissions section (lines 182-210) shows JSON format for configuring permissions, but Tauri v2 uses TOML-based capabilities files. The current example shows a plugins.device-ai-apis JSON block in tauri.conf.json which is not how Tauri v2 permissions work. Permissions should be configured via capability files in the app's capabilities directory.
design: Replace the JSON permissions example with the correct Tauri v2 approach: show how to reference plugin permissions in a capabilities TOML file (e.g., capabilities/default.json or .toml). Reference the actual permission identifiers from the permissions/ directory (device-ai-apis:allow-get-capabilities, device-ai-apis:speech-recognition, etc.). Show the actual example from examples/tauri-app/src-tauri/capabilities/default.json as a reference.
acceptance_criteria: 1. README permissions section shows correct Tauri v2 format. 2. Permission identifiers match actual permissions in permissions/ directory. 3. Instructions are clear and accurate for a developer integrating the plugin.

title: Update architecture.md stale platform status table and references
description: specs/architecture.md has several stale sections: 1) Platform Implementation Status table (lines 139-149) shows macOS as 'Stub' for all features, but macos.rs has full implementations for speech recognition, speech synthesis, OCR, barcode detection, face detection, image classification, and language identification. 2) Test count says '53 tests' which happens to be correct now but should be verified. 3) Next Steps section references 'T-26-T-28' from an old task numbering system in plan.md. 4) iOS Language ID listed as 'Stub' — needs verification against actual Swift source.
design: 1) Update macOS column: change all 'Stub' to checkmarks for implemented features. 2) Verify test count with cargo test --quiet and update if needed. 3) Replace 'T-26-T-28' reference with actual remaining work description. 4) Check iOS Swift source to verify language ID implementation status and update accordingly.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Platform status table accurately reflects implementation state. 2. Test count is current. 3. No references to old task numbering. 4. All claims in architecture.md are verified accurate.

title: macOS speech recognition hardcodes confidence to 0.95
description: In src/macos.rs, both speech_recognize_from_file (line 277) and speech_recognize_from_microphone (line 419) hardcode confidence: 0.95 in the RecognitionResult. The comment says 'SFSpeechRecognizer doesn't provide confidence per result' but SFSpeechRecognitionResult.bestTranscription has transcriptionSegments, each with a confidence property. The aggregated confidence can be computed from these.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Confidence values reflect actual recognition quality instead of static 0.95. 2. cargo test passes. 3. npm run standards passes. 4. Manual QA: run speech recognition in example app, verify confidence varies with input quality.

title: Remove leftover template code from example app
description: examples/tauri-app has leftover boilerplate from the Tauri project template: 1) src/lib/Greet.svelte — template greeting component not used by the main App.svelte. 2) src-tauri/src/lib.rs has a greet command and learn-more comment from the template.
acceptance_criteria: 1. No template boilerplate remains. 2. Example app builds and runs. 3. Tauri dev runs without errors.
